change: Change the return semantics of ValidatorSet.QuorumSize() (e.g. ceil(2N/3) -> a different threshold).

impact.why: Sole concrete implementer of the ValidatorSet.QuorumSize interface method; its formula IS the semantics being changed. (Listed because it is the impl, not the interface decl which is the seed.)
Rejects a header when len(sealers) < valSet.QuorumSize(); seal-count validity depends directly on the threshold.
Passes QuorumSize() into roundChangeSet.Add as the quorum threshold for round-change accumulation.
isJustified over justification round-changes/prepares uses QuorumSize() to accept a pre-prepare.
State advances to Prepared only when WBFTPrepares.Size() >= QuorumSize(); core liveness/safety condition.
Block is committed (commitWBFT) only when WBFTCommits.Size() >= QuorumSize(); final commit threshold.

grep_would_miss: consensus/wbft.ValidatorSet.QuorumSize (interface declaration — grep finds the name but does not reveal it is the interface contract dispatched via c.valSet)

interface-dispatch semantics: every c.valSet.QuorumSize() call resolves through the ValidatorSet interface to defaultSet; a bare grep on QuorumSize surfaces the text but not the implements relationship
notes: Only one concrete implementer (defaultSet) exists, so interface fan-out is small. cks could NOT resolve the interface-method qualified name (find_symbol returned only the two defs; impact_analysis returned empty groups) — ground truth here came from ripgrep + Read. Logging-only references at prepare.go:90/117, commit.go:93/120, engine_test.go:807 were inspected: prepare.go:117 and commit.go:120 attach the value to a logger that is then used right beside the real threshold compare, so they are folded into the prepare/commit entries; pure log/debug lines and the test were excluded. F() is a dependency OF QuorumSize, not a dependent, so excluded.
